focus: GX7 X-Metal competitor test, infomercial drivers vs OEM, quick fix culture

PAIN.quote: I purchased a group of three Gx-7's. 14,18 and 21. I am a 20 hc. None of these three measure up to my existing conventional clubs(Callaway). The driver (14) is 20 yds, shorter and dispersion is slightly less due to the shorter shots. These clubs are not worth buying

I purchased the GX7 driver. I am a 10 hdcp and am 63 years old. The shaft is way too whippy and makes it very hard to control. Not only that but I can hit my 15 yr old 5 wood 30 yrds further. I wish I hadn't purchased it
PAIN.context: 15 year old 5 wood beats new GX7

PAIN.quote: In 2014 I took $500 worth of lessons it didn't help. So I thought maybe it was the Pro. So in 2016 I tried a different Pro and for six months starting in March, I took $1,000 worth of lessons, purportedly to reduce my index by 3 strokes. Well instead it went up by 2 strokes and stayed there for nearly a year
About 10 years ago my Driver started going sideways on me, it destroyed my game and my index went way up
My buddy use to play the Alien wedge and I'd ride him all game long about it. He finally gave it away and now, years later, he still misses it
PAIN.context: Regret giving up infomercial club that worked

HOPE.quote: I am 65 years old and I hit the ball pretty far. I just purchased the GX7 and got rid of my Ping G410. The Ping was a great club and one of my golf friends is smashing the ball accurately with it. I could not hit it after much effort and assistance. I am hitting the GX-7 not as long as the Ping but way more accurately
HOPE.context: GX7 works for some - accuracy over distance

HOPE.quote: I purchased the GX7 in 2020. Needless to say I was very disappointed...BUT I am now hitting it consistently 180 yards. My HCI has dropped 5 strokes primarily due to me being able to get 'off the t-box'

I put the GX-7 in my bag in August and have played 10 rounds with it so far. On balance I really love it; it is in there to stay. My club head speed is around 90 and I hit a good GX-7 tee shot about 225

I have a wicked, consistent slice. I've taken the GX7 to the range twice and I am hitting the ball much straighter. Yes, I am loosing maybe 20 yards but I'm in the fairway most of the time. My normal drives put me in the fairway 20-25% of the time
HOPE.context: Slice gone - 20 yards shorter but fairway

HOPE.quote: Don't knock the Perfect Club: It saved the game of golf for me. Being in the fairway, about 180 yards off the tee, was HUGE for me. I was ready to quit golf, and then I could PLAY

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Scoring in Golf is all about the quality of your bad shots. A typical miss for me now with the GX-7 is a 155 yard smother hook into the left rough resulting in a bogey. A typical miss for me with my old Driver was a much more severe smother say 140 yards into the hazard or out of bounds resulting in a double or a triple
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Quality of bad shots determines score

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Using the standard deviations of ball speed and carry distance as measures of consistency, the GX-7 was less consistent across the testing pool
ROOT_CAUSE.context: GX7 actually LESS consistent in testing

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The driver achieved, on average the highest ball speeds and longest carries. Though this is to be expected as both the GX-7 and 3-wood are 43 inches

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Get Lessons. Get fitted for a real Driver. Use your 3 wood. I had done all of that and it didn't help
I did get fitted for my current $399 Driver that didn't help. I spent a lot of hours on the range and experimentation with the 3 wood off the tee, with no real success at all
If you are struggling to hit fairways then you'll be better off investing the cost of this club in lessons with a decent Pro

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: The data does not completely support the claims made in the promotional video. The results aren't likely to motivate you to lock your driver in the closet
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: GX7 doesn't live up to claims

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: While the GX-7 produced significantly shorter in distance than the driver, it finished just under four yards closer to the target line on average
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: GX7 slightly straighter but much shorter

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: This is the biggest piece of s**t I've seen. Maybe once exposed we won't have to see these stupid commercials
Basically, most of you thought this was nothing more than a snake-oil dipped ShamWoW wrapped on the end of a shaft

VILLAIN.quote: All the condescension toward these miracle clubs and infomercials should be balanced off against the establishment OEMs who have succeeded in getting idiots to pay $700 per driver and change every year to the new savior model when golf scores haven't changed in 100 years

The claims are no different. SLDR ...a faster ball speed. M1 ...TaylorMade's longest driver to date. Now Twist Face is engineered to take you farther and straighter. All over $400. That's $1200 in just a few years. Each one longer and straighter. By now everyone should be hitting it 350 down the middle
Desperate people looking for a quick fix. That's why all these swing training device makers are in business
Bottom line: if you're having trouble hitting your clubs well, no miracle club is going to help you; you'll still continue to apply your bad habits
You're absolutely right, Paulson (and others like him) has to be laughing all the way to the bank. A clubs only as good as the person using it

high_value_themes_identified: GX7 works for SOME - shorter but straighter
Quality of BAD shots determines score
Desperation for quick fix fuels entire industry
15 year old 5 wood beats new GX7
Infomercial club actually saved some golfers
Handicap dropped 5 strokes with easier club
